requirement: Improve light mode colors only, keeping the layout and component structure intact. Goals: warmer accents including logo/brandmark, restore hollow fretboard note bubbles in light mode, add a restrained warm background gradient, improve inlay visibility, and push the fretboard toward a more believable maple look without regressing dark mode.

r1.repo_shape: React 19 + TypeScript + Vite app with theme tokens in src/styles, SVG-heavy fretboard rendering in src/components/FretboardSVG, branding split across CSS and SVG components, and Playwright visual regression coverage for full app, components, mobile, overlays, and fretboard-only renders.
local_instructions: Use conventional commits and conventional PR titles.
R1 must stay in the original checkout and remain read-only.
The current branch is main tracking origin/main.
The working tree is not clean because docs/ is currently untracked.
likely_write_scopes: Theme token spine in src/styles/themes.css and src/styles/semantic.css

Fretboard material and filter tuning in src/components/FretboardSVG/FretboardDefs.tsx, src/components/FretboardSVG/FretboardBackground.tsx, and src/components/FretboardSVG/hooks/useWoodGrainTexture.ts
Brand assets in src/components/BrandMark/BrandMark.tsx, src/components/FretFlowWordmark/FretFlowWordmark.tsx, src/components/AppHeader/AppHeader.module.css, and public/favicon.svg
Light-mode contract and visual expectations in e2e/theme-contract.spec.ts and light-mode snapshot files
conflict_risks: src/styles/themes.css and src/styles/semantic.css are shared accent fan-out points for chips, controls, circle of fifths, and fretboard notes.
Light-mode fretboard notes are solid by design through both token values and explicit CSS selectors; changing only one layer will not restore hollow notes.
Branding is split between token-driven header text and hardcoded SVG fills, so partial token changes will produce mismatched brand surfaces.
Degree-color mode is a separate hardcoded path in src/core/degrees.ts and special fretboard selectors, making it easy to accidentally change theory-color behavior while fixing standard light mode.
Wood-grain turbulence and texture settings are duplicated across SVG defs and the raster texture hook, so one-sided tuning can create inconsistent maple rendering.
worktree_signals: The implementation appears to have 3-4 tightly coupled write areas but they overlap heavily through shared theme tokens and fretboard styling.
Parallelism potential is low because theme token changes, fretboard note styling, and brand updates all depend on the same light-mode palette decisions.
A sequential plan is a better fit than parallel worktrees for this requirement.
The current working tree already contains untracked docs/, which slightly increases coordination overhead if additional checkouts were introduced.
unknowns: Whether degree-color mode should stay completely independent from the warmer light-mode pass.
Whether brand SVG assets should become token-driven/adaptive or simply be retuned with new light-specific hardcoded values.
Whether warmer light-mode accents should intentionally propagate to shared controls/chips/circle surfaces or stay as localized as possible.

assumptions: Dark mode should remain behaviorally unchanged and any shared-token refactor should preserve its current output.
This work should stay color-only and should not change layout, component structure, sizing, or interaction patterns.
The existing audit document is the primary source of grounded scope for the light-mode pass.
Because write scopes overlap heavily, the likely execution shape is sequential rather than parallel.

questions.question: Should the warmer light-mode pass include degree-color mode, or should that path stay independent?
questions.why: Degree-color mode is hardcoded separately and broadening into it increases both scope and regression risk.

options.label: Leave degree-color mode unchanged
options.description: Warm standard light mode only and preserve the current theory-color palette path.

options.label: Warm degree-color mode too
options.description: Harmonize the degree-color path with the new light-mode direction, accepting broader design and test churn.

questions.question: How should the logo and wordmark be updated for light mode?
questions.why: The header text already uses theme tokens, but the SVG brand assets still hardcode neon colors.

options.label: Make SVG branding light-aware
options.description: Route the brandmark and wordmark through theme-aware colors so light and dark stay coordinated long term.

options.label: Retune SVG hardcodes only
options.description: Keep the current SVG structure and just replace the hardcoded light-facing colors with warmer values.

questions.question: Should the warmer palette propagate through shared light-mode accent surfaces, or stay as localized as possible?
questions.why: The current accent tokens feed chips, selected controls, circle highlights, and fretboard note roles from a shared semantic layer.

options.label: Propagate through shared light tokens
options.description: Use the token spine so light-mode controls and shared accents warm up together with the shell and fretboard.

options.label: Localize the warm pass
options.description: Keep shared control accents closer to their current palette and focus the warmer shift on shell, brand, and fretboard surfaces.

options.label: Propagate selectively
options.description: Warm the shell, fretboard, and brand system globally, but preserve a few shared accent surfaces if they become muddy or too low-contrast.

acceptance_criteria: Light mode uses a warmer overall shell, surface, and accent palette without changing layout or component structure.
The logo, wordmark, and related branding surfaces align with the warmer light-mode palette and remain coherent with dark mode.
Standard light-mode fretboard notes return to ring-style or hollow bubbles instead of solid fills.
Degree-color mode is intentionally warmed as part of this pass rather than left as an untouched theory-color exception.
Light mode shows a visible but restrained warm app background gradient.
The fretboard reads more like maple in light mode, with more visible inlays and tuned grain, vignette, and texture treatment.
Dark mode behavior remains unchanged unless a shared-token refactor forces a no-op structural update.
Relevant light-mode contract tests and visual snapshots are updated intentionally.

execution_strategy.rationale: Theme tokens, branding, fretboard note styling, degree-color mode, and maple-material tuning all overlap through shared files and semantic color contracts, so a one-branch sequential implementation is safer than splitting ownership.

decisions.gemini_model_policy: use gemini-3.1-pro-preview for high-risk work; use Gemini auto only when the task risk is lower

chunks.name: Warm light mode palette pass
chunks.goal: Implement the warmer light-mode pass across shared tokens, branding, fretboard note styling, degree-color mode, maple fretboard materials, and the affected tests and light-mode snapshots in one sequential branch and PR.
implementation: Retune the light-mode shell, surface, and accent token spine toward a warmer palette while preserving dark mode behavior.
Make the light-mode brandmark and wordmark theme-aware so they consume the warmer palette instead of hardcoded neon colors.
Restore hollow ring-style note bubbles for standard light mode and warm the degree-color mode path intentionally rather than leaving it untouched.
Tune the light-mode fretboard wood, grain, vignette, and inlay treatment toward a clearer maple read without changing geometry or structure.
Update the light-mode contract expectations and refresh only the affected light-mode visual snapshots.
chunks.commit: feat: warm light mode palette and fretboard materials
chunks.pr_title: feat: warm light mode palette and fretboard materials
chunks.pr_body:
Summary:
- warm the light-mode token spine and app gradient
- make branding light-aware and restore hollow fretboard notes
- tune maple fretboard materials and update affected light-mode tests/snapshots

Verification:
- npm run lint
- npm run test
- npm run test:e2e -- e2e/theme-contract.spec.ts
- npm run test:visual

Risks:
- shared semantic accent tokens also affect chips, controls, and the circle of fifths
- degree-color mode is intentionally included in this pass
- visual snapshot churn is expected in light mode only
